revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Fujishiro-ōji]][[ja:藤白神社]]
{{nihongo|'''Fujishiro Ōji Shrine'''|藤白神社||||Fujishiro Ōji|fr|Fujishiro-ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Ōji|qq=}} is a shrine that worships {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|Nigihayahi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. in {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kainan, Wakayama|lt=Kainan City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan, Wakayama|qq=}}
{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}}|image=File:241006 Fujishiro-jinja Kainan Wakayama pref Japan02s3.jpg|deity={{ill|Nigihayahi|qid=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}} |name=藤白神社|location=[[Kainan, Wakayama|Kainan]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]|coordinates={{Coord|34.144309|N|135.209392|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Rank=旧{{ill|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/県社|qq=}}|established=the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}|main hall=三間社{{ill|流造|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/流造|qq=}}|札所等={{ill|神仏霊場巡拝の道|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=}}第7番（和歌山第7番）|alternative name={{Ublist| 藤白若一王子権現 | 藤代王子 | 藤白権現 }}|festival=馬角神事、七夕祭|map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture|label=藤白神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}}|prefecture={{ill|Fujishiro|qid=Q134454573|1=Fujishiro}}|part_of={{ill|Gotai-ōji|qid=Q3111472|1=Gotai-ōji}}; {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|qid=Q3200280|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines}}|architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}}}
It is part of a pilgrimage route called the {{ill|Gotai Ōji|fr|Gotai-ōji|Ō=Ō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotai Ōji|qq=}}
* {{ill|Fujishiro Ōji|fr|Fujishiro-ōji|ja|藤白神社|Ōji=Ōji|Fujishiro-ōji=Fujishiro-ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishiro Ōji|1=Fujishiro Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3090605}} * {{ill|Kirime Ōji|fr|Kirime-ōji|ja|切目王子|Kirime Ōji=Kirime Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirime Ōji|1=Kirime Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3197362}} * {{ill|Inabane Ōji|fr|Inabane-ōji|ja|稲葉根王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabane Ōji|1=Inabane Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3149767}} * {{ill|Takijiri Ōji|fr|Takijiri-ōji|ja|滝尻王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijiri Ōji|1=Takijiri Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3514339}} * {{ill|Hosshinmon Ōji|ja|発心門王子|fr|Hosshinmon-ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosshinmon Ōji|1=Hosshinmon Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3141012}}
There used to be 99 spots on it. Called {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, but few remain now. [[File:Fujishiro-jinja_Torii.jpg|thumb|359x359px]]
== Overview == [[File:Fujishiro-jinja_shaden.jpg|thumb|'''Shaden''']]'''Fujishiro Shrine''' is {{ill|Shinto shrine|a shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Fujishiro, {{ill|Kainan, Wakayama|en|Kainan, Wakayama|qid=Q841309|lt=Kainan City|1=Kainan, Wakayama}} {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|lt=, Wakayama Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}} . {{ill|8=ja|9=Rank|Shinto shrine|en|lt=The shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 階数|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16766628}} 's former status was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|en|lt=prefectural shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} . It is the former site of '''Fujishiro Oji''', one of the five Oji that were considered special among the 99 {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}, and was also called "Fujishiro Shrine," "Fujishiro Gongen," and "Fujishiro Wakaichi Oji Gongen." {{ill|8=ja|9=藤白鈴木氏|The Fujishiro Suzuki clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Fujishiro Suzuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18224450}} has served as priests for generations, and {{ill|8=ja|9=鈴木屋敷|the Suzuki mansion|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Suzuki Yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17219449}} is said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Suzuki (disambiguation)|en|Suzuki (disambiguation)|qid=Q224928|lt=the Suzuki surname|1=Suzuki (disambiguation)}}.
== deity ==
* Main deity: {{ill|8=ja|9=ニギハヤヒ|Nigihayahi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} * Consort deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, Kumano Izumo no Kami, Kumano Hayatama no Kami, Kumano Fusumi no Kami, Haraedo no Okami
== history == The date of its founding is unknown, but it is said to have been built during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} . It is also said that the shrine building was built during {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|qq=}} 's visit {{ill|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|en|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|qid=Q11408352|lt=to Muroyu|1=Nanki-Shirahama Onsen}} . {{Sfn|長谷川|2007}} The Fujishiro Suzuki clan, the direct descendants of {{ill|10=ja|11=穂積氏|the Hozumi clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Hozumi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597079}}, whose ancestor is {{ill|Nigihayahi|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, the main deity of this shrine, have served as priests for generations as {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=a shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}} . Later, this place would become the base for spreading Kumano faith throughout the country.
During the heyday of the medieval Kumano Goko, it was revered as one of {{ill|8=ja|9=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|the five most prestigious shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3200280}} among {{ill|8=ja|9=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=The 99 shrines|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3200280}}, and was considered the No. 1 torii gate in Kumano (the entrance to Kumano) and a key location on the way to Kumano. {{ill|Jōan (era)|In the|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan (era)|qq=}} pilgrimage diary of {{ill|8=ja|9=吉田経房|Yoshida Tsunefusa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Fujiwara Tsunefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11414052}} (collected in {{ill|8=ja|9=吉記|the Yoshiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Kikki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11414309}} ), the entry for September 25, {{ill|1174|en|1174|qid=Q19690|lt=1174, states|1=1174}} that "At Fujishiro-oji, village kagura was performed," and in the pilgrimage diary of {{ill|10=ja|11=広橋経光|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20042592}} (collected in {{ill|Minkei-ki|ja|民経記|lt=the Minkei-ki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkei-ki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20042592}} ), {{ill|Jōgen (Kamakura period)|the entry|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgen (Kamakura period)|qq=}} for April 25, {{ill|1210|en|1210|qid=Q5250|lt=1210|1=1210}}, states that when visitors made pilgrimages, the Narekomai dance and village kagura dance were performed by {{ill|Miko|shrine maidens|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miko|qq=}} who were stationed at Fujishiro-oji {{Sfn|「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会|1985}} . In the pilgrimage diary of {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|Gotoba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Toba|qq=}} (collected in {{ill|8=ja|9=明月記|the Meigetsuki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Meigetsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3007406}} ), the entry for October 9, {{ill|1201|en|1201|qid=Q5225|lt=1201|1=1201}}, states that "offerings to the sutras" and "shirabyoshi" appear, and it was customary for offerings of poetry, village kagura, {{ill|Sumo|fr|lt=sumo wrestling|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumo|qq=}}, and other such events to be held. {{Sfn|平凡社地方資料センター|1997}} The road leading west from the east entrance to the temple grounds was once {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=the Kumano pilgrimage route|1=Kumano Kodō}}, and the main approach to the north is connected to the route of the early modern Kumano Kaido road. {{Sfn|「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会|1985}}
During Emperor Gotoba's pilgrimage to Kumano in 1201, a poetry gathering was held in {{ill|Yuasa, Wakayama|en|Yuasa, Wakayama|qid=Q1345625|lt=Yuasa|1=Yuasa, Wakayama}}, Fujishiro's next lodging, and the poems composed at the gathering were dedicated to Prince Fujishiro. Three copies of poems composed by Emperor Gotoba and others at this time have been preserved as Kumano Kaishi {{Sfn|平凡社地方資料センター|1997}} and are held in {{ill|Yōmei Bunko|en|Yōmei Bunko|qid=Q11658779|lt=the Yomei Bunko Collection|1=Yōmei Bunko}} and elsewhere. {{Sfn|「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会|1985}}
A document from the Zenrinji Temple dated {{ill|Ōei|1400|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei|qq=}} lists the {{ill|1400|en|1400|qid=Q6108|lt=1400|1=1400}} as " {{ill|8=ja|9=内海町_(和歌山県)|Fujishiro Oji Men" (Fujishiro Oji Domain|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Utsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11394136}} ), which indicates that the family had 3 cho 3 tan of land, the largest area of land in the Ono district. {{Sfn|平凡社地方資料センター|1997}} However, the shrine buildings and sacred grounds were lost during the civil wars of {{ill|Sengoku period|simple|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} .
In {{ill|1601|en|1601|qid=Q6725|lt=1601|1=1601}}, {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} Yukinaga donated six koku of land to the village of Fujishiro (Shoku Kii Fudoki {{Sfn|「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会|1985}} ). In addition, construction of the shrine building was carried out in the same year and {{ill|1666|en|1666|qid=Q7585|lt=in 1666.|1=1666}} {{Sfn|「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会|1985}} The Kii Zoku Fudoki, a geographical {{ill|8=ja|9=紀伊続風土記|record|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604964}} compiled by {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|qid=Q1069187|lt=the Kishu Domain|1=Kishū Domain}} in the late {{ill|Edo period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, records the shrine as Fujishiro Wakaichi Oji Gongen Shrine, and that its grounds measured 28 bays east to west and 30 bays north to south, with three main doors, an office, an offering hall, a bell tower, a stone torii gate, and three sub-shrines {{Sfn|平凡社地方資料センター|1997|qq=draft}}, and that a branch temple of Wakayama Ungai-in served as the head priest. {{Sfn|「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会|1985}}
In {{ill|Meiji period|simple|en|lt=the Meiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}} era, {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=the separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|qid=Q702298|lt=the destruction of Buddhism|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} took place, but the three wooden seated statues of Kumano Sansho Gongen Honji Buddha (designated tangible {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q858308|lt=cultural properties|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}} by Wakayama Prefecture) that were at this shrine escaped damage and remain at the shrine to this day. After the Meiji period, it was Ranked as a village shrine, then in the early {{ill|Shōwa period|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|qq=}} period it was Ranked as a town shrine, and in {{ill|1939|en|1939|qid=Q18639|lt=1939|1=1939}} (Showa 14) it was Ranked as {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|a prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} shrine. In {{ill|1909|en|1909|qid=Q2057|lt=1909|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), {{ill|8=ja|9=九十九王子_(海南市)|List_of_Kujūku_Ōji_(Kainan_City)|en|lt=Haraedo Oji|lt=Haraido Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Kainan City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370050}} was enshrined there. {{Sfn|「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会|1985}}
== Temple grounds ==
* main hall (designated as a tangible cultural property by Wakayama Prefecture) - Rebuilt in {{ill|1663|en|1663|qid=Q7577|lt=1663|1=1663}} by carpenter Nakamura Shinpei at the behest of {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|qid=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu|1=Tokugawa Yorinobu}} {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|qid=Q1069187|lt=lord of the Kishu domain|1=Kishū Domain}} . * Inner gate * Worship hall (wari-haiden) * Shrine office * {{ill|8=ja|9=鈴木屋敷|Suzuki Residence|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Suzuki Yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17219449}} - The remains of the residence of the {{ill|8=ja|9=藤白鈴木氏|Fujishiro Suzuki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Fujishiro Suzuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18224450}} clan, one of the three Kumano clans and who served as priests at Fujishiro Shrine. It was considered the head residence of the Suzuki clan throughout Japan, and its gardens are prized for their curved water style of {{ill|Heian period|simple|en|lt=the Heian period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, but the building was falling into disrepair. {{Sfn|長谷川|2007}} <ref name="Yomiuri">{{Cite web|date=2022-05-15|title=和歌山：＜海南で上棟式＞鈴木屋敷 完成へ前進|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/local/wakayama/news/20220514-OYTNT50192/|access-date=2022-06-26|website={{ill|読売新聞オンライン|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/読売新聞オンライン|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|読売新聞社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/読売新聞社|qq=}}|和書}}</ref> As a result, restoration work will begin {{ill|2021|en|2021|qid=Q49628|lt=2021|1=2021}} ( {{ill|Reiwa period|simple|lt=Reiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiwa period|qq=}} 3) <ref name="Yomiuri" /> and be completed in March {{ill|2023|en|2023|qid=Q49622|lt=2023|1=2023}} (Reiwa 5) <ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-30|title=鈴木姓のルーツとされる「鈴木屋敷」、和歌山に復元…鈴木さん親睦団体や「スズキ」も寄付金|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20230330-OYT1T50dd3/|access-date=2023-03-30|website=読売新聞オンライン|publisher=読売新聞社|和書|qq=draft}}</ref> . It has been open to the public since {{ill|April 1|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 1|qq=}} of the same year. <ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-04-03|title=鈴木屋敷を復元 姓の発祥地で式典|url=https://www.wakayamashimpo.co.jp/2023/04/20230402_dd5809.html|access-date=2023-04-03|publisher={{ill|和歌山新報|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/和歌山新報|qq=}}|和書}}</ref> ** Garden - The {{ill|8=ja|9=回遊式庭園|stroll-style pond garden|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Strolling garden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420231}} was created towards the end of {{ill|Muromachi period|simple|lt=the Muromachi period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}} .
=== Sub-shrines ===
* Fujishiro Oji Gongen main hall (藤白王子権現本堂) - Also known as Gongen Hall (権現堂). The Fujishiro Oji Gongen main hall within the shrine precincts honors Fujishiro Oji, enshrining three Buddhist statues representing the main deity. These statues were originally housed in Chudoji, the temple of Fujishiro Oji's shrine, but were relocated to associated temples during the Tenshō period (天正) 13 (1585th year ) during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's (豊臣秀吉) {{ill|8=ja|9=紀州征伐|conquest of Kishu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10855721}}, and later restored during the Edo period (江戸時代). They survived the separation of Shinto and Buddhism (神仏分離) during the Meiji era and have been preserved to this day{{Sfn|Hasegawa|2007}}. The statues, all from the late Heian period, include the following main ones{{Sfn|Hasegawa|2007}}: ** Wooden seated statue of the Honjibutsu of the Three Kumano Gongen Deities (木造熊野三所権現本地仏坐像) - Wakayama Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property *** Wooden seated statue of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}} (阿弥陀如来) - Honjibutsu of Ketomi Mikoto. *** Wooden seated statue of Yakushi Nyorai (薬師如来) - Honjibutsu of Kumano Hayatama no Okami. *** Wooden seated statue of {{ill|8=ja|9=千手観音|Senju Kannon|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Thousand-armed Kannon|qid=Q10904910}} (千手観音) - Honjibutsu of Kumano Fusumi no Okami. ** Wooden standing statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|qid=Q11404731|lt=Juichimen Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (十一面観音) - Wakayama Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property - Honjibutsu of Fujishiro Wakaichi Oji. ** Wooden statues of Vaishravana (毘沙門天) and {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} (不動明王) Triad - Guardians of the entrance to Kumano. * Arima no Miko Shrine (有馬皇子神社) - Enshrined deity: {{ill|8=ja|9=有間皇子|Arima no Miko|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Arima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11516721}} (有間皇子). Executed by strangulation by Emperor Tenji (天智天皇) on Fujishiro Hill nearby. * Ebisu Shrine (恵比寿神社) - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}} (恵比寿). Originally located in Nadaka, Horai-cho, where there was a market, it was relocated to this shrine in the Meiji period. * Mi Shrine (巳神社) * {{ill|8=ja|9=祇園神社|Gion Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|qid=Q11588442}} (祇園神社) * Akiba Shrine (秋葉神社) * Sumiyoshi Shrine (住吉神社) * {{ill|Shiogama Jinja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiogama Jinja|qq=}} (塩釜神社) * Komori Kusunoki Shrine (子守楠神社) - Enshrined deity: Kumano Tsuno Hinomikoto. Minakata Kumagusu received the characters "熊" and "楠" for his name from this shrine within the precincts of Fujishiro Oji. The character "藤" seen in the names of his siblings was also given by this shrine, with prayers for the child's safe growth under the deity's protection. This tradition originates from the faith in the kusunoki tree. The custom of receiving names from Kusunoki Shrine as the guardian deity of twenty-four surrounding villages is also mentioned in the "Kii Province name Guide" (紀伊国名所図会){{Sfn|Iikura|2006|qq=draft}}.
== Cultural Assets ==
=== Nationally designated historic site ===
* Fujishiro Oji ruins - The temple grounds and the grounds of the Suzuki residence are designated.
=== Wakayama Prefecture designated tangible cultural property ===
* main hall - {{ill|April|fr|Avril|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April|qq=}} 23, {{ill|1997|en|1997|qid=Q2088|lt=1997|1=1997}} <ref>{{Cite web|last=和歌山県教育委員会|title=県指定文化財・有形文化財・建造物|url=http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/mokuroku/kenkenzo.html|access-date=2009-04-07|和書|qq=draft}}</ref> * Three wooden seated statues of Kumano Sansho Gongen Honji Buddhas - Designated on May 31, {{ill|2005|en|2005|qid=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 17) <ref name="Wakayama_Art">{{Cite web|last=和歌山県教育委員会|title=県指定文化財・有形文化財・美術工芸品|url=http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/mokuroku/kenbizyutu.html|access-date=2009-04-07|和書|qq=draft}}</ref> . * Wooden standing statue of Eleven-Headed Kannon - Designated on May 31, {{ill|2005|en|2005|qid=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 17) <ref name="Wakayama_Art" /> .
=== Wakayama Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property ===
* Fujishiro Lion Dance - A {{ill|Lion dance|en|Lion dance|qid=Q713866|lt=lion dance|1=Lion dance}} and Sarutahiko no Mikoto wearing a tengu mask sing of the tranquility of spring. In addition to performing the first dance at the signal of the New Year's Eve bell, they also perform a dance in the town at the autumn festival on October 15th. {{Sfn|「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会|1985}} Designated on April 12th, {{ill|1966|en|1966|qid=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}} (Showa 41) <ref>{{Cite web|last=和歌山県教育委員会|title=県指定文化財・民俗文化財|url=http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/mokuroku/kenminzoku.html|access-date=2009-04-07|和書|qq=draft}}</ref> . The target is the Fujishiro Lion Dance Preservation Society.
=== Wakayama Prefecture designated historic site ===
* Haraedo Oji ruins
== festivals ==
* October 15th - Autumn festival * November dd - Prince Arima festival
== Nearby and nearby temples ==
; {{ill|8=ja|9=神仏霊場巡拝の道|Pilgrimage route to sacred places of Shinto and Buddhist sites|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10444949}} : 6 {{ill|Dōjō-ji|en|Dōjō-ji|qid=Q1272708|lt=Dōjō-ji|1=Dōjō-ji}} * '''7 Fujishiro Shrine''' - 8 {{ill|Kamayama Shrine|en|Kamayama Shrine|qid=Q11597743|lt=Kamayama Shrine|1=Kamayama Shrine}}
== Transportation facilities ==
* Approximately 20 minutes walk from Kainan Station on the JR West Kisei Main Line
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Fujishiro-jinja_shaden.jpg|alt=藤白神社 社殿|Fujishiro-jinja Shaden (Shrine Building) File:Fujishiro-jinja_Torii.jpg|alt=藤白神社 鳥居|Fujishiro-jinja Torii (Shrine Gate) File:Fujishiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|alt=藤白神社03|Fujishiro-jinja 03 File:Fujishiro-jinja04s3200.jpg|alt=藤白神社04|Fujishiro-jinja 04 File:藤白神社.jpg|alt=藤白神社|Fujishiro-jinja </gallery><gallery> File:Fujishiro-jinja_shaden.jpg|link=File:Fujishiro-jinja_shaden.jpg|alt=拝殿|Worship hall File:Fujishiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|link=File:Fujishiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|alt=旧社務所・茶屋跡|Former shrine office and teahouse site File:藤白王子権現.jpg|link=File:藤白王子権現.jpg|alt=藤白王子権現|Fujishiro Oji Gongen File:Fujishiro-jinja04s3200.jpg|link=File:Fujishiro-jinja04s3200.jpg|alt=楠の巨木|Giant camphor tree </gallery>
== See Also ==
